figures.caption: Figure 5.2: Per-timestep velocity RMSE (↓, left) and SSIM (↑, right) on FIT across 15 autoregressive steps (3 rounds). FlowRefiner (red) maintains the lowest RMSE and highest SSIM throughout the entire rollout horizon.
Figure 5.1: u-component predictions on FIT at Round 3, showing the z=60 slice with magnified insets over a high-frequency region.
Figure D.3: Pressure (p) predictions on FIT at autoregressive Round 3. Layout as in Figure 5.1.
Figure D.2: w-component predictions on FIT at autoregressive Round 3. Layout as in Figure 5.1.
Figure 5.3: Physical consistency on FIT at Round 3. Velocity RMSE vs. four physics metrics; lower-left is optimal.
Table C.1: Autoregressive prediction on TGV (64×128×128). Per-channel RMSE (↓) and SSIM (↑) across two rounds. Baselines from [5]. Bold: best, underlined: second best.
Figure D.1: v-component predictions on FIT at autoregressive Round 3 (t=6.0), with magnified insets over a high-frequency turbulent region. Layout as in Figure 5.1.

Figure C.1: Angle-averaged velocity energy spectra E(k) for FIT (1283) and TGV (64×1282). FIT follows the Kolmogorov k−5/3 slope across a broad inertial range, while TGV concentrates energy in lowwavenumber modes and decays sharply at higher k.

Figure 3.1: FlowRefiner overview. (a) Predictive leap followed by flow refinement in state space. (b) Pipeline: the base model (k=0) produces an initial prediction; K refinement steps inject noise and integrate the FM velocity field with a decoupled sigma schedule.

Table 4.1: Autoregressive prediction on FIT isotropic turbulence (1283). Per-channel RMSE (↓) and SSIM (↑) across three rollout rounds. Deterministic baselines from [5]. Bold: best, underlined: second best.
